message:
docs(mvcc): flag Silo EpochGroupCommit wiring as a deferred decision (bd-rh3sr)

The `silo_epoch` module doc already noted the primitive is scaffold-only
and "not yet wired" into real commit paths. Extend that note with the
concrete reason wiring has NOT been done yet — so the next person who
opens this file does not unilaterally stack Silo on top of the existing
`fsqlite_pager::GroupCommitQueue` / `GroupCommitConsolidator` pipeline
that already provides same-process WAL group commit.

Trace of the investigation behind the note (archived in bd-rh3sr):

- `grep -rn "EpochGroupCommit::\|silo_epoch::" crates/` — zero callers
  outside `silo_epoch.rs` tests. IMPL-16 (bd770f2f, 2026-04-19) is
  scaffold-only as the module doc already admitted.
- `SimpleTransaction::commit` (fsqlite-pager/src/pager.rs:8143) flushes
  frames through `GroupCommitQueue` with `decide_group_commit_arrival_wait`,
  fairness budgets, starvation guards, and global consolidation metrics.
  Wiring Silo without removing this would double-batch.
- 2026-04-23 8-thread profile (profiling-handoff-20260423T155542Z/
  campaign-summary.md) shows zero WAL-append / fsync / GroupCommit
  symbols in the top-10; the 1→2-thread cliff on CPU is dominated by
  btree cell-parse (~24.9%), MVCC page-lock wait (~7%),
  PublishedPagerState lifecycle (~9%). The cliff is plausibly off-CPU
  page-lock wait, not WAL-append serialization.

No behaviour change. No cargo check / clippy / test impact — pure
module-level doc update. Intent: prevent a speculative "wire Silo" PR
from landing without the off-CPU profile that should drive the
tune-vs-replace-vs-delete decision captured in bd-rh3sr.

Bead: bd-rh3sr

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
